breakdown: Tests have descriptive names and clear docstrings explaining edge cases, but lack type annotations and use dynamic class creation. The architecture demonstrates good separation with test helpers and scenario runners, though error handling patterns aren't visible in test code. Type safety is weak—no TypedDict, dataclasses, or generics employed.
reasoning: As partial test code, I can't assess naming of production functions or error handling in main logic. Type safety is clearly low with no annotations. Tests themselves are well-structured and documented, justifying higher test coverage and documentation scores, but the absence of type safety significantly drags the total down.
